calendar.matters:
Petition To Set Aside Trust, For Relief From Financial Elder Abuse, And For Declatory Relief
Off Calendar Jan-11-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom: Dave Karlinski, Esq. for Michelle Matranga (Petitioner) also appearing via Zoom. Franchesca Callejo, Esq. (Guardian ad Litem) GAL for Minors Francesca Matranga & Frangeo Matranga). Amanda Ebey, Esq. for Esperanza Matranga (Respondent).

The Court addressed the five matters on calendar and stated that it appreciated everyone's good work. Mr. Karlinski stated that they have no issues with the other petition if it can be wrapped up globally; no objection as long as the settlement is approved under CCP 664.6. Ms. Ebey had nothing to add. Ms. Callejo stated that she would "second" counsels' comments. The Court noted that all counsel had reviewed the settlement agreement. In response to Mr. Karlinski's comment, the Court stated that if it were to change its position that it would restore all of the petitions and the trial to calendar.

There being no additional comments, the Court orders the following:

Michelle Matranga's Petition to Set Aside Trust, for Relief from Financial Elder Abuse, and for Declaratory Relief - off calendar;
Esperanza Matranga's Petition for Order Confirming Trust Assets (Probate Code 850) - under submission;
Esperanza Matranga's Petition for Approval of Settlement Agreement Pursuant to CCP 664.4 - under submission with the intent to grant.
GAL Franchesca Callejo's Petition for Approval of Compromise of Claim or Action or Disposition of Proceeds of Judgment for Minor or Person with a Disability - under submission with the intent to grant;
Trial - off calendar.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Deputy: Singh. Matter not reported.

Off Calendar Nov-02-2022 Continued To Nov-30-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Approval Of Compromise Of Claim Or Action Or Disposition Of Proceeds Of Judgment For Minor Or Person With A Disability
Off Calendar Sep-07-2022 Continued To Nov-02-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Dave Karlinsky appeared by zoom for and with Michelle Matranga (petitioner) by zoom.
Amanda Ebey appeared by zoom for Esperanza Matranga.
Franchesca Callejo (guardian ad litem for minors Francesca and Frangeo) appeared by zoom.

The court stated that at the last hearing it asked counsel to explain what the statutory authority grounding the petition is, what the relevant legal standard is, to identify the key terms of the proposed settlement, the parties understanding of what court approval would mean, what the parties think the effect of the court approving this is, and that it didn't get that. Discussion was held regarding CCP 664.4 not being a provision that authorizes petitions to approve a settlement, the reference of 664.4 being a typo in the caption, but the text in the petition making reference to 664.6, there being other provisions in the probate code that talk about approval of settlements that set forth what the standards might be and then explain in writing how the settlement benefits the estate, how the paragraph entitled "benefits of settlement to minor beneficiaries" discusses the benefit to the minor children and to the trust, the settlor having made no provision for the continuing expenses of the trust administration, who would remain responsible for the maintenance of the property during the life time of the life tenant, Ms. Callejo's petition addressing the issues between the remainderman and the life tenant, the issue of whether or not the court can order transfer of the real property back to 2018 and whether it has authority to that, there being confusion as to where Ms. Callejo's fees as the guardian ad litem would come from (the trust or out of the $176,00.00 to each of the minors), the difficulty in determining Ms. Callejo's fees, the court's concern regarding the provision that the mom can petition for sums out of the minor settlements for support, how funds belonging to minors are generally not available for ordinary care and support, the issue of whether or not this compromise puts the minors in a worse position, how the terms of this settlement are identical to those in the settlement that was hammered out by Commissioner Lee in 2021, Ms. O'Keeffe's (decedent's estate planning attorney) declaration that supports that this settlement comports with the decedent's intent/wishes, the manner in which the minor's funds can and cannot be invested, etc. The court informed counsel that section 8 (where the fees are coming out of) in the minor's compromise order needs to be addressed , that section 9 needs to identify the banks, that it needs the birthdates of the minors in 9-C-2 and that the second to the last sentence needs to reflect trustee. The Petition for Approval of Compromise of Claim, etc., the Petition to Set Aside Trust, etc., the Petition for Order Confirming Trust Assets, the Petition for Approval of Settlement Agreement Pursuant to CCP 664.4 and the trial are continued to November 2, 2022 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Ms. Callejo is to file an amendment regarding her fees. Ms. Ebey and Mr. Karlinsky are to provide authority on the issue of back dating transfer of the property.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Singh. Not reported.

Petition To Set Aside Trust, For Relief From Financial Elder Abuse, And For Declatory Relief
Off Calendar May-17-2022 Continued To Sep-07-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Dave Karlinsky, via Zoom, for Michelle Matranga, Petitioner; Amanda Ebey, via Zoom, for Esperanza Matranga, Objector; Franchesca Callejo, via Zoom, Guardian Ad Litem.

The court and counsel discuss the status of this matter and the petition for minor's compromise filed on May-13-2022 which is set for hearing on Sep-07-2022. Ms. Callejo reports that an amended settlement agreement is attached to the petition for minor's compromise and included in the supplement to the petition for approval of settlement agreement filed on May-12-2022. The court confirms with counsel that all matters on today's calendar should be continued to be heard alongside the petition for minor's compromise.

All matters are continued to Sep-07-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Gomez. Not Reported.

Petition For Approval Of Settlement Agreement Pursuant To Ccp 664.4
Off Calendar May-17-2022 Continued To Sep-07-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Trial
Off Calendar May-17-2022 Continued To Sep-07-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Approval Of Settlement Agreement Pursuant To Ccp 664.4
Off Calendar Feb-15-2022 Continued To May-17-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Amanda Ebey appeared by zoom for Esperanza Matranga (respondent).
Dave Karlinsky appeared by zoom for Michelle Matranga (petitioner).
Franchesca Callejo (guardian ad litem for the minors) appeared by zoom.

The court noted that at the last calling of the matter three months ago it made it clear that it would grant one additional continuance for submission of an amended petition for settlement, a petition for minor's compromise and for the parties to discuss settlement, but it doesn't have either one of those petitions. The parties reported that they have had meetings and are working on getting information to Ms. Callejo, that the holdup appears to be with the appraisal company, that Ms. Ebey is working with her client regarding the investments of the $160,000.00 for each child, that Ms. Callejo has conferred with Ms. O'Keeffe (estate planning attorney) to get her understanding of what the parties were trying to do, that Ms. Callejo cannot approve the settlement without information regarding the valuation of the property and specific information regarding the investment plan, etc. The court stated that council for the parties all need to work together to get this to a resolution, that this is coming up on three years, that it's too long for this case to be sitting around and that the trial was set five months ago. The court also stated that this needs to be made a priority, otherwise it is going to send the parties to Dept. 206, that it wants to see in the amended petition what the statutory authority for the petition is, what the relevant legal standard is, what the material terms are of the settlement and what the parties think the effect of the court approving this settlement is. The Petition for Approval of Settlement Agreement Pursuant to CCP 664.4, the Petition to Set Aside Trust, etc. and the trial are continued to May 17, 2022 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The parties need to work together on coming up with a guardian ad litem for the minors.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Cristobal. Not reported.

Petition For Order Confirming Trust Assets

Petition For Approval Of Settlement Agreement Pursuant To Ccp 664.4
Off Calendar Nov-16-2021 Continued To Feb-15-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Dave Karlinsky, via ZOOM, for Michelle Matranga, Petitioner; Amanda Ebey, via ZOOM, for Esperanza Matranga, Respondent; Franchesca Callejo, via ZOOM, Guardian Ad Litem.

The court points counsel to the number of times this matter has been on calendar and the court still does not have a joint trial plan or at the very least clarification from the parties regarding the status of the case and reminds the parties that this matter will not be able to settle without the participation of the Guardian Ad Litem. The court advises that it is not inclined to approve the settlement as is and instructs the parties to meet and confer regarding settlement. The court advises that a petition for minor's compromise will be required as the minors should not have to wait until they are thirty years old for distributions. The court advises that it is willing to grant one additional continuance for a submission of an amended petition for settlement, a petition for minor's compromise, and for the parties to discuss settlement. Ms. Callejo advises that she is happy to meet with counsel to discuss the court's concerns.

After additional discussion, the matters on today's calendar are continued to Feb-15-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204. The parties are allowed to calendar the petition for minor's compromise on Feb-15-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204 and are advised to include a note to the clerk that the setting has been approved by the judge in open court, when the petition is submitted for e-filing.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Franchesca Callejo, via ZOOM, Guardian Ad Litem, Amanda Ebey, via ZOOM, for Esperanza Matranga, Objector; Dave Karlinsky, via ZOOM, for Michelle Matranga, Petitioner.

The court begins by advising the parties that they have not complied with several case management orders. The court also points to the issue of engaging in settlement discussions without involving the Guardian Ad Litem. Ms. Callejo reports that she has seen some of the documents in this matter but will need time to do an in-depth review of the papers and the settlement. Ms. Callejo advises that it appears as though she has all the pleadings in the matter except for the Petition to Confirm Trust Assets. Ms. Callejo also waives notice in open court. The court instructs Ms. Ebey to provide Ms. Callejo with a copy of the Petition to Confirm Trust Assets. The court asks Ms. Callejo to file her report one week prior to the next calling of this matter.

The Case Management Hearing is off calendar. The remaining matters are continued to Nov-16-2021 at 9:00 am in Department 204.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Dave Karlinsky, via ZOOM, for Michelle Matranga, Petitioner; Amanda Ebey, via ZOOM, for Monroy Matranga, Respondent.

The court advises counsel that the court is not content with the information submitted by the parties and requests an update regarding settlement discussions in this matter. Ms. Ebey reports that the parties met with Settlement Officer Pang Ly and agreed to a conditional settlement and that a Petition for Approval of Settlement is in the e-filing queue. The court advises that the parties are expected to file and ex parte application for appointment of a Guardian ad Litem sooner rather than later. The court then queries counsel if there are any objections to the 850 petition. Mr. Karlinsky reports that he has no objection.

After brief discussion, the Trial, Case Management Hearing, Petition to Set Aside Trust, etc., and Petition for Order, etc., are continued to August 17, 2021, at 9:00 am in Department 204.

Petition To Set Aside Trust, For Relief From Financial Elder Abuse, And For Declatory Relief
Off Calendar Apr-27-2021 Continued To May-11-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Case Management Hearing
Off Calendar Apr-27-2021 Continued To May-11-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Dave Karlinsky, via ZOOM, counsel for Michelle Matranga, Petitioner; Amanda Ebey, via ZOOM, counsel for Esperanza Monroy Matranga, Respondent.

At the outset, the court requests an update from counsel regarding settlement discussions. Mr. Karlinsky reports that a global settlement offer has recently been proposed and requests a short continuance to report back to the court regarding the status of the offer. Ms. Ebey replies that the offer is not enough. The court then suggests that perhaps scheduling a settlement conference with Commissioner Pang Ly may help the parties with settlement. The parties agree and the court advises them to contact Commissioner Ly to schedule a settlement conference. The court shall prepare a Notice of Settlement Conference.

After additional discussion, the Case Management Hearing and Petition to Set Aside Trust, etc., are continued to May 11, 2021, at 9:00 am in Department 204.

Judge: Hon. Mary Wiss. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Tauscher. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Case Management Hearing
Off Calendar Mar-23-2021 Continued To Apr-27-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Dave Karlinsky appeared by telephone for Michelle Matranga (Petitioner).
Amanda Ebey appeared by zoom for Esperanza Matranga (Respondent).

The Court asked if the parties have reached a settlement, how they would like to proceed and noted that there is no Guardian Ad Litem ("GAL"). Ms. Ebey stated that they have not been able to reach a settlement and that they are still discussing the matter. Mr. Karlinsky stated that if they reach a settlement, they will be putting in a request for the Court to appoint a GAL, and suggested a three or four-week continuance to make a determination if settlement is likely. The Petition to Set Aside Trust, etc. and the case management hearing are continued to April 27, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Vedica Puri. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Probate Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

Petition To Set Aside Trust, For Relief From Financial Elder Abuse, And For Declatory Relief
Off Calendar Dec-29-2020 Continued To Mar-09-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Dave Karlinsky, Esq. for Petitioner Michelle Matranga and Amanda Ebey, Esq. for Respondent Esperanza Matranga

This matter comes on calendar for Case Management on Petition to Set Aside Trust for Relief from Financial Elder Abuse and for Declaratory Relief. The Court received the joint status report on December 17, 2020. Counsel provide further updates to the Court. Mr. Karlinsky indicates that they are moving forward with a settlement offer that is generally along the lines of what the parties discussed at the mediation held last March. Counsel is cautiously optimistic that they will be able to present an offer within the first two weeks of the new year. Counsel also takes this opportunity to apologize for failure to comply with the Court's recent order. Ms. Ebey confirms that the trial date is still set in case the settlement proposal is unsuccessful. The Court confirms that the last Case Management Conference is set for March 9, 2021 at 9:00 am in Department 204. Court stands adjourned.
Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Jones, Deputy: N. Ng. Matter Not reported.

Petition To Set Aside Trust, For Relief From Financial Elder Abuse, And For Declatory Relief
Off Calendar Sep-08-2020 Continued To Dec-08-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Dave Karlinsky appeared by zoom for Michelle Matranga (Petitioner) and specially appearing for Attorney Amanda Ebey.

The Court noted that it read the status report that was filed by Esperanza. Mr. Karlinsky stated that he did not file a status report, that he filed a request for a continuance, but it got kicked back and that the parties are cautiously optimistic that they have reached settlement. Mr. Karlinsky also requested a 60-day continuance to finalize the agreement and present it to the Court for approval. The Court informed Mr. Karlinsky that it is issuing a case management order. The case management hearings (2) are set for December 8, 2020 and March 9, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The trial is set for June 8, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. Karlinsky is to monitor the Court's register of actions to obtain a copy of the order and serve it on everyone who is entitled to service. The Court informed Mr. Karlinsky that if the settlement involves a Minor, they will need a Guardian Ad Litem and that there is no way around that. The Petition to Set Aside Trust, etc. is continued to December 8, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

Petition To Set Aside Trust, For Relief From Financial Elder Abuse, And For Declatory Relief
Off Calendar Dec-17-2019 Continued To Mar-17-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Dave Karlinsky for Michelle Matranga (Petitioner).
Amanda Ebey for Esperanza Matranga (Respondent).

The Court asks where things are and how the parties wish to proceed. Ms. Ebey stated that they are well on their way, that discovery has been propounded, that she would like to take a couple of depositions and that they are amenable to mediation. The Parties agreed to participate in mediation. The Court signed the order referring the matter to Pro Bono Mediation based on the representation of Ms. Ebey that the value of the trust is below $3,000,000.00. Mediation must be completed no later than February 28, 2020. The Parties are to notify the Probate Examiner as to the outcome of mediation by March 4, 2020. The Petition to Set Aside Trust, etc. is continued to and the Parties are to return on March 17, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of mediation. The Parties are also to file a joint status report by March 4, 2020. If the Parties come to the realization that the trust does not come in under $3,000,000.00, they will have to make arrangements for private mediation.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.

Petition To Set Aside Trust, For Relief From Financial Elder Abuse, And For Declatory Relief
Off Calendar Nov-05-2019 Continued To Dec-17-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:

Dave Karlinsky, counsel for Petitioner Michelle Matranga.

The Court notes the absence of Respondent's counsel, Amanda Ebey, for which neither Mr. Karlinsky nor the clerk have explanation. Mr. Karlinsky reports on the status of case, noting that some interrogatories have been propounded to Respondent, a second set will be forthcoming, and he anticipates propounding document production requests to third parties by way of subpoena. The Court continues the matter to December 17, 2019 by which date all discovery must be propounded. Counsel are to meet and confer and report to the Court on how this matter should be resolved, whether by alternative dispute resolution or trial.

Judge: Hon. Peter J. Busch; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Reporter: Not Reported. (D204)
